meta.reportLabel: Mon - OIL PUSHES HIGHER STILL: Sunday's Hormuz break extended and BROADENED. Crude +3% - Brent tops $90, WTI ~$83.5 (1-mo highs) - as the US-Iran war ran a 6th straight day of strikes (no ceasefire; Iran 'no plans for negotiations') AND a drone strike at Iraq's Basra terminal suspended ALL Iraqi crude loading, stacking a second choke on Hormuz. USD stays firm (Sept-hike odds ~56-62%, DXY ~100.9); CAD keeps its petro tailwind; JPY the clean oil-loser pinned at the MoF 162 line. Gold coiled at $4,000 (~$4,001); silver outperforms (~$56.8, +1.6%). CAD/JPY LONG (HIGH); USOIL LONG (MED). Two binaries: NZ Q2 CPI TONIGHT (22:45Z, seen ~4.0-4.1%, consensus cooled) and a live MoF strike at 162.
meta.coverage: USD, EUR, GBP, JPY, AUD, NZD, CAD · 18 pairs + DXY + JPYBASKET + GER40 + XAU + XAG + USOIL
meta.regime: Sunday's oil breakout extended and broadened on Monday, hardening the new trade map. Crude jumped ~3% - Brent topped $90, WTI back to ~$83.5 (one-month highs) - as the US-Iran war ran into a sixth straight day of exchanged strikes with no ceasefire progress, and the supply squeeze broadened beyond Hormuz: a drone strike at Iraq's Basra terminal suspended crude loading at all Iraqi export terminals, stacking a second choke on the ~20%-of-world-flow Hormuz blockage. The cascade runs on: the oil bid keeps the US inflation channel armed (Sept-hike odds ~56-62%, DXY ~100.9), hands the net-energy-exporter CAD its terms-of-trade tailwind, and hammers the ~100%-importer JPY pinned at the MoF 162 line. Short USD/CAD stays neutralised (both oil-inflation winners); CAD/JPY LONG stays the cleanest two-leg trade (HIGH).
meta.nextBigEvent: The Iran-US war / oil supply squeeze is the dominant driver: Hormuz largely blocked (~20% of world oil), a 6th day of strikes, and now a second choke as Iraq's Basra terminal is shut - Brent >$90. A sudden Oman/ceasefire breakthrough crushes the oil/CAD/gold premium in minutes; further escalation re-spikes it. Then NZ Q2 CPI TONIGHT (Jul 20 22:45Z, seen ~4.0-4.1% - consensus cooled from 4.4-4.5%) is the NZD swing, with Jul 23 ECB and Jul 28-29 FOMC framing the back half of the month.

dailyRead: The oil breakout that flipped the board on Sunday extended and broadened on Monday, hardening every leg of the new trade map. Crude jumped ~3% to start the week - Brent topped $90, WTI back to ~$83.5 (one-month highs) - as the US-Iran war ran into a sixth straight day of exchanged strikes with no ceasefire progress (Pakistan and Qatar mediating behind the scenes; Iran publicly says it has 'no plans for negotiations'). The supply squeeze broadened beyond Hormuz: a drone strike at Iraq's Basra terminal suspended crude loading at all Iraqi export terminals, stacking a second choke on top of the ~20%-of-world-flow Hormuz blockage. The cascade runs on: the oil bid re-arms the US inflation channel (Sept-hike odds ~56-62%, DXY ~100.9), hands the net-energy-exporter CAD its terms-of-trade tailwind, and hammers the ~100%-importer JPY, pinned at the MoF's 162 line. Gold is consolidating right at $4,000 (~$4,001) while silver outperforms (~$56.8, +1.6%) as its industrial leg firms. Cleanest leans hold and firm: CAD/JPY LONG (HIGH); USOIL LONG (MED) as the squeeze broadens; short the high-betas vs CAD (AUD/CAD, NZD/CAD SHORT, MED); gold RANGE->long tilt. Two live binaries: NZ Q2 CPI tonight (22:45Z) - consensus cooled to ~4.0-4.1% (a narrower hot-risk) - and USD/JPY at 162 is a live MoF-strike zone; size the yen-shorts for an intervention snap.

today.headline: Oil tailwind extends - Brent tops $90 and a fresh Iraqi supply shock broadens the squeeze; CAD/JPY LONG stays HIGH
today.read: Monday's push to Brent >$90 plus the Iraqi Basra shutdown deepen exactly the scenario Macklem flagged ('hikes if oil spikes'), so CAD keeps a genuine net-exporter terms-of-trade boost. CAD/JPY LONG (HIGH) is the cleanest two-leg trade; short the high-betas vs CAD (AUD/CAD, NZD/CAD). Risk: a ceasefire crushes oil AND flips the BoC dovish; USD/CAD stays a two-way, not a short.

today.headline: The clean oil-shock loser deepens - a ~100% importer pinned near 162 as Brent tops $90; MoF verbal-only so far
today.read: The extending oil spike is a direct terms-of-trade shock; USD/JPY has repeated 163 failures and steady bids at 162, the MoF's line (verbal warnings only; the Y11.73tn April precedent unused). JPY is the weakest currency - CAD/JPY LONG the clean expression. Risk: a stealth or overt MoF strike snaps the yen higher fast and hits every XXX/JPY carry-long.

today.headline: Q2 CPI prints TONIGHT (22:45Z) - and consensus cooled to ~4.0-4.1% (ANZ 4.0%), a narrower hot-risk
today.read: The swing is imminent but the hot-risk is narrower than last week: consensus is now ~4.0-4.1%, only at/just above the RBNZ's 3.9% call. A hot beat still re-arms the hawks; a sub-4% print validates the dovish-hike. The oil-shock risk-off tail + a firmer USD cap the high-beta NZD; NZD/CAD SHORT holds on the oil leg. Risk: an oil-shock flush; a ceasefire.

today.headline: No change - the soft-USD slide stays over; the oil-inflation channel keeps the no-cut trade armed (DXY ~100.9)
today.read: Higher oil keeps feeding US inflation, holding Sept-hike odds ~56-62% and DXY ~100.9 into the Jul 28-29 FOMC - a hawkish lean holding its firmer footing. Short USD/CAD stays neutralised (both oil-inflation winners). Risk: a sudden oil de-escalation deflates the inflation scare and re-softens the USD; a soft payroll re-arms the slide.

today.headline: No change - the oil terms-of-trade hit + a firm USD both weigh; EUR/USD leans lower into Jul 23 ECB
today.read: As a large energy importer, the extended oil spike is a deepening terms-of-trade drag, and the firmer dollar compounds it - EUR/USD leans lower. Jul 23 ECB is the next test. Risk: a hawkish ECB core tone (core 2.5%) hands it a leg; an oil de-escalation lifts the relief bid.

today.headline: No change - firm rate story (services 3.7%), but a high-beta passenger to the oil shock; UK June CPI ~Jul 22
today.read: Services CPI 3.7% keeps both hike dissents alive into Jul 30, but a firmer USD and an oil-shock risk-off tail cap the cable tailwind. GBP/USD RANGE->short tilt; GBP/JPY carry-long tilt holds. Risk: a risk-off flush; a firmer USD; a soft UK CPI (~Jul 22).

today.headline: No change - oil-shock risk-off + firm USD cap it on both sides; Jun jobs ~Jul 23
today.read: Sticky-core floor, but the oil-shock risk-off tail and the firmer USD cap it both ways. Thin Aug hike tail (~36%), China CPI +1%. Short vs CAD (AUD/CAD). Risk: a re-escalation flush re-softens it; a hot Q2 CPI (late Jul) + a ceasefire re-arm the hawks.

strength.verdict: STRONGEST — the petro-currency's oil tailwind extends as Brent tops $90
strength.tag: Brent >$90 + Iraqi supply shock; USD/CAD a two-way
strength.drivers: May CPI 3.2% (gasoline-driven; ex-gas 2.2%, core ~2.0%); Jun jobs +18.2k beat (unemp 6.5%); BoC held 2.25% (6th straight) - Macklem: hikes possible IF oil spikes. Monday's Brent >$90 plus the Iraqi Basra shutdown deepen exactly that scenario, so the conditional-hawkish hold keeps live upside and CAD keeps a genuine net-energy-exporter terms-of-trade boost.
strength.forward: 🦅 The extending oil spike keeps the BoC's oil-conditional hold live and CAD's real terms-of-trade tailwind; CAD/JPY LONG stays HIGH. But short USD/CAD stays neutralised - both are oil-inflation winners now. Risk: a ceasefire crushes oil AND flips BoC dovish; a risk-off flush hits CAD's beta leg. Next BoC Sep (data-dependent).

strength.verdict: FIRMER — the oil-inflation channel keeps the no-cut trade armed
strength.drivers: June CPI -0.4% m/m -> 3.5% y/y (from 4.2%); core FLAT m/m -> 2.6%. NFP +57k (Jun); claims 208k. Fed 3.50-3.75% hold. The oil spike keeps feeding US inflation, so the soft-USD slide stays over: Sept-hike odds ~56-62% and DXY ~100.9 into the Jul 28-29 FOMC.
strength.forward: 🦅⚖️ Higher oil -> higher US inflation -> Fed-stays-high: a hawkish lean holding its firmer footing. That keeps short USD/CAD neutralised. Risk: a sudden oil de-escalation deflates the inflation scare and re-softens the USD; a soft payroll re-arms the slide. Next FOMC Jul 28-29.

strength.verdict: FIRM — own rate story, but a high-beta passenger to the oil shock
strength.drivers: Core 2.6% (May); services CPI 3.7% (up from 3.2%); headline 2.8% held; DMP wages 4.2%. BoE 3.75%, hawkish 7-2 hold (2 hike votes). A firmer USD and an oil-shock risk-off tail cap the cable tailwind; GBP is a high-beta passenger.
strength.forward: 🦅⚖️ Services re-acceleration keeps the hike tail and both dissents alive into Jul 30, but a firmer USD + risk-off cap the near-term tailwind. Risk: a risk-off flush; UK June CPI (~Jul 22) is the check; a firmer USD. Next BoE Jul 30.

strength.verdict: FIRM — Q2 CPI tonight, but consensus cooled and oil-risk-off caps it
strength.drivers: RBNZ hiked 25bp to 2.50% Jul 8, 6-0, scripted dovish (headline seen PEAKED at 3.9% Q2). Consensus for Q2 CPI tonight has cooled to ~4.0-4.1% (ANZ 4.0%) - at/just above that call, a narrower hot-risk than last week's 4.4-4.5% chatter. The oil-shock risk-off tail + a firmer USD cap the high-beta NZD.
strength.forward: 🦅⚖️ The Jul 20 22:45Z Q2 CPI is the swing - a hot beat (>4%) re-arms the hawks; a sub-4% print validates the dovish-hike. Risk: an oil-shock flush hits high-beta NZD; a ceasefire. Next RBNZ Aug 19.

strength.verdict: SOFTER — oil terms-of-trade hit + a firm USD weigh
strength.tag: energy importer; leans lower into ECB
strength.drivers: EZ June flash HICP 2.8% (down from 3.2%) BUT core ROSE to 2.5% from 2.2%. Unemp 6.3%. ECB 2.25% depo (hiked Jun 11), meeting-by-meeting. As a large energy importer, the extended oil spike is a deepening terms-of-trade drag, and the firmer dollar compounds it - EUR/USD leans lower.
strength.forward: ⚖️🔻 Oil terms-of-trade hit + a firm USD = a lower lean into Jul 23 ECB. Risk: a hawkish ECB core tone (core 2.5%) hands EUR a leg; an oil de-escalation lifts the relief bid. Next ECB Jul 23.

strength.drivers: May CPI: trimmed mean 3.6%; headline 4.0%; unemp ~4.3% loosening (June jobs ~Jul 23). RBA 4.35% (held Jun after 75bp of 2026 hikes); Aug 11 hike priced only ~36%. China CPI missed at +1%. Sticky-core floor, but the oil-shock risk-off tail and the firmer USD cap it on both sides.
strength.forward: 🔻⚖️ Oil-shock risk-off + firm USD cap = two-way; short vs CAD. Risk: a re-escalation flush re-softens it; a ceasefire + a hot Q2 CPI (late Jul) re-arm the hawks. Next RBA Aug 11.

strength.verdict: WEAKEST — the oil-shock terms-of-trade loser at the MoF 162 line
strength.drivers: Core CPI 1.4% (May, subsidy-suppressed); unemp 2.5%, jobs/applicants 1.26 tight. BoJ 1.00% (hiked Jun 16, 7-1), guides more hikes. As a ~100% energy importer, the extending oil spike is a deepening terms-of-trade shock - USD/JPY is pinned near 162 with repeated 163 failures, testing the MoF's 162 line (verbal warnings only so far; Y11.73tn April precedent unused).
strength.forward: ⚖️🦅 The deepening oil-shock hit pins the yen at the MoF line; BoJ Jul 31 hawkish is the only offset. Risk: a stealth or overt MoF strike snaps the yen higher fast; a genuine panic breaks the carry the other way. Next BoJ Jul 31.

symbols.why: The clean oil-divergence, the two-leg move hardens: Brent >$90 + the fresh Iraqi supply shock boost net-exporter CAD (BoC oil-hawk live) and hammer the ~100%-importer JPY (pinned at the MoF 162 line).
symbols.risk: A ceasefire crushes oil and CAD; a MoF strike snaps the yen higher fast; a broad risk-off flush hits CAD's beta leg.

symbols.why: The squeeze broadens: WTI ~$83.5 (one-month highs), Brent tops $90 (+3% Mon); a drone strike at Iraq's Basra terminal halted ALL Iraqi crude loading on top of the Hormuz blockage. Premium is real and widening.
symbols.risk: A sudden Oman/ceasefire breakthrough unwinds the premium in minutes; demand-destruction fear + OPEC+/Saudi spare capacity cap the top.

symbols.why: Oil-winner CAD vs an oil-shock-hit high-beta AUD: the extended spike lifts petro-CAD and the risk-off tail + firm USD cap AUD - the oil leg splits them cleanly.
symbols.risk: A ceasefire crushes oil and re-pins CAD; a hot AU Q2 CPI (late Jul) lifts AUD; a risk-on melt-up.

symbols.why: Oil-winner CAD vs a risk-off-capped NZD - the oil leg leads, though tonight's NZ Q2 CPI (~4.0-4.1%) is a narrowing binary.
symbols.risk: A ceasefire; a hot NZ Q2 CPI beat lifts NZD; a risk-on melt-up.

symbols.why: The extending oil spike + firmer USD both push it up, but it is pinned at the MoF 162 line with repeated 163 failures - the ceiling is policy, not price. A long tilt capped by the MoF.
symbols.risk: A MoF strike snaps it lower fast; an oil de-escalation caps the top; a soft-USD resumption.

symbols.why: The old star short stays neutralised: both are oil-inflation winners now - CAD as the petro-currency, USD via the Fed-stays-high channel (Sept odds ~56-62%) - a two-way near ~1.38-1.40.
symbols.risk: A ceasefire crushes oil and flips BoC dovish (USD/CAD up); a soft US payroll re-softens the USD (USD/CAD down).

symbols.why: Consolidating right at $4,000 (~$4,001) - the haven + inflation-hedge bid holds it, but the firmer USD and higher real rates cap upside; a coiled two-way with an upward lean.
symbols.risk: A hawkish FOMC repricing lifts real rates; a ceasefire deflates the geopolitics leg; a fresh escalation re-fires it.

symbols.why: Oil-importer EUR terms-of-trade hit + a firmer USD (Sept odds ~56-62%) - leans lower into Jul 23 ECB. A short tilt.
symbols.risk: A hawkish ECB Jul 23; an oil de-escalation lifts the EUR relief bid.

symbols.why: Firm-rate GBP but a firmer USD + risk-off tail trim the cable - high-beta passenger. A short tilt.
symbols.risk: A hawkish BoE read-through; an oil de-escalation softens the USD; UK CPI ~Jul 22.

symbols.why: Oil-loser JPY firms the carry, but the oil-shock risk-off + firm USD cap the high-beta AUD - offsetting. A mild long tilt.
symbols.risk: A MoF strike lifts JPY; an oil-shock flush hits AUD faster.

symbols.why: Weak-JPY carry + CPI-tonight NZD, but risk-off + firm USD cap the base. A mild long tilt.
symbols.risk: An oil-shock flush hits NZD; a sub-4% NZ CPI; a MoF strike.

symbols.why: Firm-rate GBP vs the oil-loser JPY at 162 - carry-long tilt holds, capped by the MoF line and risk-off.
symbols.risk: A MoF strike snaps JPY up; a risk-off flush hits GBP.

symbols.why: Both energy-importers hit by oil, but the yen is the weaker leg at the MoF line - mild carry-long tilt.
symbols.risk: A MoF strike; a hawkish ECB Jul 23; an oil re-spike hits both.

symbols.why: Sticky-core AUD vs a firmed USD + the oil-shock risk-off tail - the mild long tilt is capped. A short tilt.
symbols.risk: A ceasefire risk-on melt-up; a soft USD; Jun jobs ~Jul 23.

symbols.why: CPI-tonight NZD vs a firmed USD + risk-off cap. A short tilt.
symbols.risk: A hot NZ Q2 CPI beat lifts NZD; an oil de-escalation softens the USD.

symbols.risk: A sub-4% NZ CPI lifts EUR; a hawkish ECB Jul 23.

symbols.why: Both high-beta and oil-risk-capped, but NZD carries the tonight CPI catalyst - mild short tilt.

symbols.why: Firm GBP vs a doubly-capped AUD - mild long tilt.
symbols.risk: A hot AU CPI; a risk-on melt-up lifts AUD; BoE Jul 30.

symbols.why: Firm GBP vs an NZD with a live CPI-tonight binary - two firm legs, edge thin.

symbols.why: Holds ~100.9 as the oil-inflation channel keeps the no-cut trade armed (Sept-hike odds ~56-62%); the soft-USD slide stays over - a firmer bias into the FOMC. A long tilt.
symbols.risk: A sudden oil de-escalation deflates the inflation scare; a soft payroll re-arms the slide.

symbols.risk: A MoF intervention strike hands JPY a fast bid and spikes the basket; a genuine risk-off panic.

symbols.why: The extended oil spike deepens the energy-cost headwind for the record-stretched (~25,800) melt-up, and a firmer USD + a still-hot war compound it - leans lower.

symbols.why: Silver outperforms gold (~$56.8, +1.6%) as the industrial-demand leg firms alongside the haven bid - the decoupling shows; capped by any oil-shock growth-fear.
symbols.risk: An oil-shock risk-off leg hits industrial demand; a gold-led haven re-fire lifts it; a firmer USD.

catalysts.event: Strait of Hormuz - largely blocked; 6th day of strikes
catalysts.note: Iran asserts control of the strait (~20% of world oil flow); traffic sharply reduced; US-Iran trade fire for a 6th straight day with no ceasefire progress - every headline moves oil, CAD, JPY, gold, risk.

catalysts.event: Iraq Basra terminal drone strike - all Iraqi crude loading suspended
catalysts.note: A NEW supply choke stacked on top of Hormuz; broadens the squeeze beyond Iran and is the fresh Monday catalyst behind Brent >$90.

catalysts.event: Trump's Hormuz toll + US naval blockade
catalysts.note: Blockade of Iranian ports in force; a demanded toll on Hormuz cargo - a structural supply-cost shock while the strait is contested.

Pakistan and Qatar mediating behind the scenes, but Iran says 'no plans for negotiations'; the June 60-day MOU is dead - a breakthrough crushes the oil/CAD/gold premium, a further escalation re-spikes it.
Consensus cooled to ~4.0-4.1% (ANZ 4.0%), at/just above RBNZ's 'peaked 3.9%' call; a hot beat re-arms the hawks, a sub-4% print validates the dovish-hike.
Hold; core-2.5% is the Sept-hike signal but the oil terms-of-trade hit clouds it.
Unemployment ~4.3% loosening; feeds the Aug 11 RBA calculus.
Warsh round 2; hold; the oil-inflation spike keeps Sept-hike odds ~56-62%.
Dissent count for GBP after services 3.7%.
Guidance with spot ~162 at the MoF line; the oil bill is a growing drag.

geopolitics: Sunday's oil breakout extended and broadened on Monday - the single most important development on the board. The US-Iran war ran into a sixth consecutive day of exchanged strikes with no imminent ceasefire: US strikes hit Iranian command centres and missile sites, Iran targeted US bases in Gulf states, and diplomacy is only quiet (Pakistan and Qatar working to restart talks) while Tehran publicly insists it has 'no plans for negotiations.' The Strait of Hormuz - through which ~20% of the world's oil normally moves - remains largely blocked, with Iran asserting control and traffic sharply reduced. The decisive fresh Monday catalyst was a second choke opening up: a drone strike at Iraq's Basra terminal suspended crude loading at all Iraqi export terminals, stacking a new supply shock on top of Hormuz. The market paid for it: crude jumped ~3% - Brent topped $90 and WTI returned to ~$83.5 at one-month highs. FX transmission (Monday - the new map hardens): (1) The USD stays firm - higher oil feeds straight into US inflation, keeping the Fed-stays-high trade armed: Sept-hike odds ~56-62% and DXY ~100.9 into the Jul 28-29 FOMC. (2) CAD, the net-energy-exporter, extends its oil tailwind - the exact scenario Macklem flagged ('hikes if oil spikes') - but because the USD is also firm, short USD/CAD stays neutralised (both are oil-inflation winners) and remains a two-way. (3) The JPY is the clean loser - a ~100% energy importer taking a deepening terms-of-trade shock, pinned near 162 at the MoF's line (verbal warnings only so far), which keeps CAD/JPY LONG the cleanest two-leg trade (HIGH). (4) Gold is consolidating at $4,000 (~$4,001) on the haven + inflation-hedge bid, a RANGE->long tilt capped by the firmer USD and real rates, while silver outperforms (~$56.8, +1.6%) as its industrial leg firms. (5) The high-betas (AUD, NZD) and the importer EUR are hit by the oil-shock risk-off tail + the firmer USD - short vs CAD. (6) GER40 stays an energy-cost headwind. The one landmine cutting across every yen-short: USD/JPY at 162 is a live MoF-strike zone - an intervention would snap the yen higher in minutes and hit CAD/JPY and every XXX/JPY carry-long. Trade the oil-divergence, but keep the yen-short sizing intervention-aware.
